question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: extractTokensRelationshipQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply alternating asc and desc order values across many concurrent requests, causing the database query planner to switch between forward and reverse index scans, potentially invalidating cached query plans and increasing per-query latency enough to delay block ingestion by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getTokenRelationships()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TokenRelationshipViewModel construction is performed in a synchronous .map() loop after the async DB call, and by requesting the maximum limit of token relationships cause the CPU-bound mapping loop to block the Node.js event loop long enough to delay processing of block-ingestion callbacks by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getTokenRelationships()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a timeout on TokenService.getTokenAccounts() to send a request that triggers a slow query (e.g., full table scan via wide token.id range), and if the query runs long enough, hold a DB connection for the duration of a block interval, effectively delaying block ingestion by 500% or more of the average block time?
